title: Restoring exported runc container requires `--runtime runc`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Importing a checkpoint from a container using `--runtime runc`  cause `Error: OCI runtime error: CRIU restoring failed -52.`

`restore.log` ends with: 

```
(00.006773) mnt:                Will mount 638 from /
(00.006776) mnt:                Will mount 638 @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.1PjNRd/13-0000000000/proc/scsi
(00.006778) mnt:        Read 638 mp @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.1PjNRd/13-0000000000/proc/scsi
(00.006789) Error (criu/mount.c:2830): mnt: No mapping for /proc/sched_debug mountpoint
```

Restoring from a non exported checkpoint works. Then the `restore.log` looks like this:

```
(00.000885) mnt:                Will mount 638 from /
(00.000887) mnt:                Will mount 638 @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.8DLWUa/13-0000000000/proc/scsi
(00.000889) mnt:        Read 638 mp @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.8DLWUa/13-0000000000/proc/scsi
(00.000894) mnt:                Will mount 637 from /dev/null (E)
(00.000896) mnt:                Will mount 637 @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.8DLWUa/13-0000000000/proc/sched_debug
(00.000898) mnt:        Read 637 mp @ /tmp/.criu.mntns.8DLWUa/13-0000000000/proc/sched_debug
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run --name loop -d --runtime runc alpine sh -c "while true; do date; sleep 1; done"`
2. `podman container checkpoint --export /tmp/loop.tar.zst loop`
3. `podman rm loop`
3. `podman container restore --import /tmp/loop.tar.zst`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: OCI runtime error: CRIU restoring failed -52.`

**Describe the results you expected:**

To be restored and continue running. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Everytime

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 19:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Version: 3.16

comments.body:
Please try: `podman --runtime runc container restore --import /tmp/loop.tar.zst`

This is a bug. If you use runc to checkpoint the container Podman should automatically select the same runtime during restore.
I am not sure how we can address it.  `--import` and `--export` do not store what runtime was used

> I am not sure how we can address it. `--import` and `--export` do not store what runtime was used

I had a look at it and the used runtime is actually part of the checkpoint (config.dump: `"runtime": "crun",`). I tried to create a patch, but the problem is that the runtime is selected very early in the code path and once we unpack the checkpoint archive the runtime is already selected.

I was not sure if I can just change the name and path of an existing runtime. It felt not like the right thing to do.

My main problem was that unpacking the checkpoint (only one file (config.dump) would be needed, however) seems like a heavy operation and doing it twice felt like a lot of overhead.

If we could change the runtime properties (mainly name and path) once it has been instantiated it would be easy to solve.
I'm glad closing this as there's a working solution for the issue, maybe just add a note to the documentation and call it a day?

@adrianreber I think we need to check if the runtime requested by the checkpoint exists in our available runtimes - if it does not, we can give a sane error back to the user ("checkpoint was created using "runc" but Podman does not recognize this runtime" or similar.
